1.name: Easy
1.description: Factual questions directly from the text

2.name: Medium
2.description: Interpretive questions about meaning and themes

questions.question: What was David's condition at the beginning of 1 Kings?

options.B: Old and stricken in years, unable to get warm

questions.explanation: David was elderly and could not keep warm despite being covered with clothes.

questions.question: What was the name of the young woman chosen to care for King David?
questions.explanation: Abishag was a fair damsel from Shunem chosen to care for the aging king.

questions.question: Who exalted himself, saying he would be king?
questions.explanation: Adonijah prepared himself with chariots and horsemen to make himself king.

questions.question: Who were NOT with Adonijah in his attempt to become king?
questions.explanation: Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, Benaiah, and Solomon remained loyal to David.

questions.question: Who did Nathan advise to save her life and Solomon's life?
questions.explanation: Nathan warned Bath-sheba that Adonijah was trying to be king without David knowing.

questions.question: What oath did David swear to Bath-sheba?

options.A: Solomon would be king and sit on his throne

questions.explanation: David swore that Solomon would reign after him and sit on his throne.

questions.question: Where did David order Solomon to be anointed king?
questions.explanation: David instructed that Solomon be taken to Gihon and anointed there as king.

questions.question: Who anointed Solomon king?

options.D: Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet

questions.explanation: Zadok took oil and anointed Solomon while Nathan the prophet was present.

questions.question: Where did Adonijah flee when he heard Solomon was made king?
questions.explanation: Adonijah grabbed the horns of the altar in fear.

questions.question: What did David do as his days drew near to an end?
questions.explanation: David charged Solomon his son as he approached death.

questions.question: What did David tell Solomon to keep and do?

options.A: The charge of the LORD, to walk in his ways and keep his statutes

questions.explanation: David urged Solomon to keep the charge of the LORD and walk in His ways.

questions.question: How long did David reign over Israel?

options.B: Forty years (seven in Hebron, thirty-three in Jerusalem)

questions.explanation: David reigned a total of forty years over Israel.

questions.question: What happened to Solomon's kingdom after David's death?
questions.explanation: Solomon's kingdom was established greatly on the throne of David.

questions.question: How did Solomon respond to Adonijah's request for Abishag?
questions.explanation: Solomon saw this as a bid for the throne and ordered Adonijah killed.

questions.question: What punishment did Solomon give Abiathar the priest?
questions.explanation: Solomon exiled Abiathar to his own fields in Anathoth.

questions.question: What was Joab's fate?

options.D: He was made captain of the host

questions.explanation: Benaiah killed Joab at the altar and buried him in the wilderness.

questions.question: Who did Solomon appoint over the host instead of Joab?
questions.explanation: Solomon made Benaiah captain of the host and Zadok became priest.

questions.question: What did God offer Solomon at Gibeon?

options.A: To ask for whatever he desired

questions.explanation: God appeared to Solomon in a dream and asked what he desired.

questions.question: What did Solomon ask God for?

options.D: An understanding heart to judge his people

questions.explanation: Solomon asked for wisdom to discern between good and evil in leading his people.

questions.question: What did God promise Solomon in response to his request?

options.D: The ability to conquer the world

questions.explanation: God gave Solomon wisdom, and also riches and honor beyond all kings.

questions.question: What was the dispute between the two women Solomon judged?

options.C: Over a living child each claimed as her own

questions.explanation: Two harlots came with a dispute over which one the living child belonged to.

questions.question: How did Solomon determine the true mother?

options.A: By her willingness to give up the child rather than see it harmed
options.D: By having each woman testify under oath

questions.explanation: The true mother would rather give up her child than see it divided, showing true love.

questions.question: What was Solomon's chief officer?
questions.explanation: Azariah was Solomon's principal officer and governor.

questions.question: How was Judah described during Solomon's reign?

options.C: As many as the sand by the sea in multitude, eating and drinking and making merry

questions.explanation: Judah enjoyed great peace and prosperity under Solomon.

questions.question: What request did Solomon send to Hiram, king of Tyre?

options.A: To become his ally in war
options.B: For cedar and fir trees for building the temple
options.D: For workers to farm the land

questions.explanation: Solomon requested timber to build the house of the LORD.

questions.question: What was the main purpose Solomon had in mind for cedar?

options.A: To build a house for the name of the LORD

questions.explanation: Solomon wanted to build a magnificent temple for the LORD.

questions.question: In which year of Solomon's reign was the temple begun?
questions.explanation: The temple construction began in the fourth year of Solomon's reign.

questions.question: What were the dimensions of the house Solomon built for the LORD?

options.B: Sixty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high
options.D: One hundred twenty cubits in height

questions.explanation: The temple was sixty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high.

questions.question: What did Solomon do while the temple was being built?

options.A: Fulfilled the word of the LORD

questions.explanation: Solomon fulfilled what the LORD had spoken concerning the house.

questions.question: What had been brought into the temple?

options.D: The things David his father had dedicated, silver and gold and vessels

questions.explanation: David's treasures and dedicated items were brought into the temple.

questions.question: Who did Solomon gather before the LORD when the temple was completed?

options.B: The elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes

questions.explanation: Solomon assembled the elders and leaders of Israel to dedicate the temple.

questions.question: What filled the house when the ark was brought in?
questions.explanation: The glory of the LORD filled the house in the form of a cloud.

questions.question: What did Solomon do before the altar of the LORD?

options.A: Spread his hands and blessed the assembly

questions.explanation: Solomon stood before the altar and spread out his hands in prayer.

questions.question: What did the LORD appear to Solomon about after the temple and palace were built?

options.B: His prayer, heard and accepted, with conditions

questions.explanation: God acknowledged the temple and Solomon's prayer with conditions for continued blessing.

questions.question: What did God say about the temple?

options.A: He had sanctified it and would dwell there

questions.explanation: God blessed the temple and said His eyes and heart would be there forever.

questions.question: Why did the Queen of Sheba come to Solomon?

options.D: To hear of his fame and test him with hard questions

questions.explanation: She came to prove him with difficult questions about his wisdom.

questions.question: What impressed the Queen of Sheba about Solomon?

options.B: His wisdom, his building, his food, his servants, and the ascent to the house of the LORD

questions.explanation: She was amazed at all of Solomon's accomplishments and magnificence.

questions.question: What did the Queen of Sheba give to Solomon?

options.C: Gold, spices, and precious stones in great abundance

questions.explanation: She gave him gifts of gold, spices, and precious stones.

questions.question: How much gold did Solomon receive yearly?
questions.explanation: Solomon received 600 talents of gold every year.

questions.question: What did Solomon do that was contrary to God's law?
questions.explanation: Solomon took many strange women, transgressing God's commands.

questions.question: What did Solomon's wives cause him to do?

options.D: Do that which was evil in the sight of the LORD

questions.explanation: His wives turned his heart away from God to idolatry.

questions.question: What did God threaten to do because of Solomon's idolatry?
questions.explanation: God said He would rend the kingdom from Solomon.

questions.question: Who was Jeroboam?

options.C: A mighty man of valor who became a servant in Solomon's house

questions.explanation: Jeroboam was a valiant servant of Solomon.

questions.question: What did the prophet Ahijah tell Jeroboam?

options.A: The kingdom would be torn from Solomon and ten tribes given to him

questions.explanation: Ahijah prophesied that Jeroboam would rule ten tribes of Israel.

questions.question: Where did all Israel go to make Rehoboam king?
questions.explanation: Israel assembled at Shechem to crown Solomon's son Rehoboam.

questions.question: What advice did Rehoboam reject?

options.A: To lighten the burden upon the people

questions.explanation: Rehoboam rejected the elders' counsel to ease the people's burden.

questions.question: What did Israel say when Rehoboam refused to lighten their burden?

options.C: What portion have we in David? To your tents, O Israel!

questions.explanation: Israel rejected Rehoboam and departed, leaving only Judah.

questions.question: Who became king over Israel after the division?

options.B: A judge from the tribe of Benjamin

questions.explanation: The ten tribes made Jeroboam their king.

questions.question: What did the man of God from Judah cry against the altar at Bethel?

options.B: A child would be born to the house of David named Josiah who would burn the bones of the priests

questions.explanation: He prophesied against the altar in the name of the LORD.

questions.question: Why did Jeroboam send his wife to Ahijah the prophet?

options.A: To inquire about their sick son

questions.explanation: Their son Abijah was ill and Jeroboam wanted to know if he would recover.

questions.question: Why did David's servants seek a young woman for him?

options.A: To cherish him and provide warmth through companionship
options.B: To serve as his nurse only

questions.explanation: They sought Abishag to care for David and help keep him warm.

questions.question: What did Nathan's intervention reveal about political strategy in the kingdom?

options.B: Wise counsel and alliance with the king's mother could counter Adonijah's plot
options.D: The kingdom was controlled by priests

questions.explanation: Nathan strategically involved Bath-sheba to influence David's decision.

questions.question: Why was Adonijah's feast interrupted and what did it signify?

options.C: Solomon was anointed king, showing Adonijah's plan had failed
options.D: A messenger brought news of war

questions.explanation: Solomon's anointing at Gihon interrupted Adonijah's coronation feast.

questions.question: What does David's charge to Solomon reveal about succession?

options.B: Being a man requires spiritual obedience and wisdom
options.D: The throne is secured by fear

questions.explanation: David emphasized righteousness and faithfulness to God's law.

questions.question: Why did David remind Solomon about Joab and Amasa?

options.B: To ensure they would be rewarded
options.D: To indicate they had unjustly murdered rivals and must face consequences

questions.explanation: David sought justice for blood they shed unlawfully.

questions.question: What does Solomon's dream at Gibeon suggest about God's availability?

options.A: God appears to those who seek Him in worship and prayer
options.C: Dreams are unreliable sources of God's will
options.D: God ignores the requests of kings

questions.explanation: Solomon was worshiping at Gibeon when God appeared to him.

questions.question: What does Solomon's request for wisdom reveal about his values?

options.C: He prioritized justice and discernment for governing

questions.explanation: Solomon valued the ability to judge rightly more than riches or long life.

questions.question: Why did God give Solomon more than he asked for?

options.B: His wise priorities pleased God, who then added material blessings
options.C: God wanted Solomon to be greedy

questions.explanation: God was pleased with Solomon's wisdom and added riches, honor, and longevity.

questions.question: What principle of justice does Solomon's judgment of the women illustrate?

options.A: True love and compassion reveal identity and truth
options.B: Compromise is always the best solution
options.C: Legal claims are the only way to judge

questions.explanation: Solomon saw that the true mother's love would compel her to sacrifice.

questions.question: What does the description of Judah's peace indicate about Solomon's earlier reign?

options.B: He achieved stability and prosperity through wise governance

questions.explanation: Judah's abundance and joy reflected Solomon's successful rule.

questions.question: Why did Solomon need cedar from Tyre?

options.C: To construct the temple, a project beyond Israel's resources

questions.explanation: Solomon's temple required superior cedar timber that only Tyre could provide.

questions.question: What did the completion of the temple signify?

options.A: The fulfillment of God's word to David
options.D: A new era of idol worship

questions.explanation: The temple's completion fulfilled the divine promise to David.

questions.question: What was the theological significance of the cloud filling the temple?

options.B: God's presence consecrated and dwelt in the temple
options.C: It was a sign of judgment

questions.explanation: The cloud represented God's glory and His presence in the temple.

questions.question: What condition did God attach to His blessing of Solomon?

options.D: Solomon and his people must walk before God in truth and keep His commandments

questions.explanation: God's continued blessing depended on obedience to His law.

questions.question: Why was the Queen of Sheba amazed at Solomon?

options.A: His wisdom and accomplishments exceeded the reports she had heard
options.C: He had defeated her in battle

questions.explanation: Solomon's wisdom, building, organization, and devotion surpassed her expectations.

questions.question: What does the world seeking Solomon's wisdom suggest about his reputation?

options.B: His renown extended beyond Israel, and many sought his counsel

questions.explanation: Kings from all lands came to hear of Solomon's wisdom.

questions.question: Why was Solomon's love of foreign women spiritually dangerous?

options.A: It was just a personal preference
options.C: These women brought idolatry and turned his heart from God

questions.explanation: Foreign wives introduced pagan worship that corrupted Solomon's faith.

questions.question: What does God's judgment on Solomon indicate about divine justice?

options.A: Even kings are accountable to God's moral law

questions.explanation: Solomon's wisdom did not protect him from judgment for his disobedience.

questions.question: What was the fatal flaw in Rehoboam's decision?

options.B: He chose harshness over wisdom, ignoring the counsel of elders

questions.explanation: Rehoboam's arrogance in rejecting wise counsel led to the kingdom's division.

questions.question: How did Israel's rejection of Rehoboam fulfill God's word?

options.A: God had promised through Ahijah that ten tribes would go to Jeroboam
options.C: The people acted without God's involvement

questions.explanation: The division occurred as God had prophesied through His prophet.

questions.question: What did the prophecy against Jeroboam's altar reveal about judgment?

options.B: God would immediately destroy the altar
options.C: God's judgment would come through a future descendant, not immediately

questions.explanation: The prophet spoke of Josiah, centuries later, fulfilling the word.

questions.question: Why did Jeroboam turn to Ahijah instead of his priests?

options.D: He recognized that Ahijah was a true prophet of the LORD

questions.explanation: Despite his rebellion, Jeroboam knew Ahijah spoke God's word.

questions.question: What made Jeroboam different from David in God's eyes?

options.B: He did not seek God with all his heart as David did

questions.explanation: Unlike David's whole heart devotion, Jeroboam turned to idolatry.

questions.question: What characterized Asa's reign as contrasted to his father Abijam?

options.A: Asa's heart was perfect toward God
options.B: Asa was unfaithful like his father
options.D: Both father and son were equally wicked

questions.explanation: Asa walked in the way of David, doing what was right before God.

questions.question: How evil was Ahab?

options.D: More evil than all kings before him, selling himself to do evil

questions.explanation: Ahab provoked God to anger more than any king before him.

questions.question: What does Elijah's sudden appearance suggest about God's response to Ahab's evil?

options.C: God sent a prophet to confront and warn Ahab
options.D: Elijah was acting alone without God

questions.explanation: God raised up Elijah as a counterforce against Ahab's idolatry.

questions.question: How did God provide for Elijah during the drought?

options.B: Through ravens who brought him bread and meat

questions.explanation: God miraculously sustained Elijah in the wilderness.

questions.question: Who did God appoint to sustain Elijah during the famine?
questions.explanation: God directed Elijah to a poor widow who would be blessed by his presence.

questions.question: What miracle did Elijah perform for the widow's son?
questions.explanation: Elijah interceded and the boy's life returned to him.

questions.question: What did Elijah demand of the people at Mount Carmel?

options.B: Destroy the prophets of Baal immediately

questions.explanation: Elijah challenged Israel to decide whom they would serve.

questions.question: What convinced the people that the LORD was God?

options.B: Fire from heaven consuming Elijah's sacrifice while Baal's altar remained unconsumed
options.C: The appearance of a sign in the sky

questions.explanation: The supernatural display of God's power converted the people.

questions.question: Why did Elijah flee to Mount Horeb?

options.B: He wanted to see the sights
options.C: He was following the king's orders
options.D: Jezebel threatened his life after the Carmel incident

questions.explanation: Elijah feared for his life and sought refuge in God's presence.

questions.question: How did God reveal Himself to Elijah at Horeb?

options.C: Not through wind, earthquake, or fire, but through a still small voice

questions.explanation: God's gentle voice was more powerful than dramatic displays.

questions.question: What did the man of God tell Ahab about God's power?

options.B: God is God of the hills and also of the valleys; He is all-powerful
options.D: God would never defeat the enemy

questions.explanation: The prophet reminded Ahab that God's power is limitless.

questions.question: Why was Ahab upset about Naboth's vineyard?

options.A: He wanted it for a vegetable garden near his palace
options.C: He wanted to build a monument

questions.explanation: Ahab coveted his neighbor's land for personal use.

questions.question: What did Jezebel do to help Ahab get Naboth's vineyard?

options.C: She arranged for false witnesses to accuse him of blasphemy, leading to his execution
options.D: She convinced the priests to take his land

questions.explanation: Jezebel orchestrated murder and perjury to take the vineyard.

questions.question: What judgment did Elijah pronounce on Ahab for his crime?

options.B: Dogs would lick his blood where they licked Naboth's
options.D: His son would become king immediately

questions.explanation: Elijah pronounced poetic justice—the same fate as his victim.

questions.question: Why were the prophets unreliable in counseling Ahab?

options.A: They were false prophets telling him what he wanted to hear, not God's truth
options.B: They were unfamiliar with military strategy
options.D: They were enemies of the king

questions.explanation: Ahab's court prophets compromised truth to please the king.

questions.question: What vision did Micaiah give Ahab about the battle?

options.D: Israel scattered like sheep without a shepherd, meaning death and defeat

questions.explanation: Micaiah prophesied truthfully that Ahab would fall in the battle.

questions.question: How did Ahab die?

options.A: Shot by an arrow at Ramoth-gilead as he hid in his chariot
options.C: He died of old age at home

questions.explanation: Ahab was mortally wounded in battle, fulfilling Elijah's prophecy.

questions.question: How did Nathan's strategy to involve Bath-sheba demonstrate understanding of human nature and palace politics?

options.B: It appealed to maternal love and David's past commitment to ensure her cooperation
options.D: It was purely manipulative without basis

questions.explanation: Nathan wisely leveraged Bath-sheba's interest in her son's welfare and David's sworn oath.

questions.question: Why does David emphasize the law of Moses as the foundation for Solomon's success?

options.A: Spiritual obedience is the only lasting basis for power and legitimacy
options.C: The law was just a cultural tradition
options.D: Solomon did not need to follow God

questions.explanation: David taught that God's law, not military or political power, sustains a kingdom.

questions.question: Why does Solomon's request for wisdom at Gibeon foreshadow his entire reign?

options.C: His wisdom would be the defining characteristic, enabling justice and prosperity

questions.explanation: Solomon's first act—seeking wisdom—established the tone for his successful early rule.

questions.question: What does the judgment of the two women reveal about the nature of true judgment?

options.B: True justice requires understanding human nature and the difference between genuine love and self-interest

questions.explanation: Solomon recognized that love manifests in sacrifice, revealing the true mother.

questions.question: Why was the fourth year of Solomon's reign the right time to build the temple?

options.A: Solomon had established his kingdom's stability and could now undertake this major project
options.B: It was the earliest possible moment

questions.explanation: Solomon waited for his kingdom to be secure before undertaking the temple.

questions.question: What theological paradox does Solomon express in his prayer?

options.A: God can fit inside a building
options.C: God, though infinite and beyond the heavens, chooses to dwell with His people in the temple
options.D: God is distant and unconcerned with the temple

questions.explanation: Solomon affirms both God's transcendence and His desire to be present with His people.

questions.question: What conditions did God attach to His permanent blessing of the temple?

options.B: Obedience to His commandments; disobedience would result in judgment
options.C: Only the priests needed to obey
options.D: The king alone had to obey

questions.explanation: God's blessing depended on the entire nation's faithfulness.

questions.question: Why does the Queen of Sheba's journey foreshadow a broader pattern?

options.A: It shows that Solomon's fame extended beyond Israel to foreign lands, validating his wisdom
options.B: It proves he was weak militarily
options.C: It indicates he would conquer other nations
options.D: It was a random event without significance

questions.explanation: Solomon's renown attracted world leaders, confirming his unparalleled wisdom.

questions.question: Why does Solomon's fall through foreign wives demonstrate a spiritual principle?

options.A: Wisdom alone is sufficient protection against sin
options.B: Personal relationships have no spiritual significance
options.D: Unequal relationships and idolatry can corrupt even the wisest person

questions.explanation: Solomon's tragic flaw shows that wisdom does not guarantee obedience or protection from spiritual corruption.

questions.question: What does God's judgment on Solomon reveal about the nature of covenant and accountability?

options.A: Covenant means unconditional support regardless of behavior
options.B: Covenant includes both blessing and curse; unfaithfulness invites judgment
options.C: The law applies only to common people
options.D: Kings are exempt from God's standards

questions.explanation: Even the greatest king must answer to God's moral law.

questions.question: Why was the assembly at Shechem critical to the kingdom's fate?

options.A: It was the moment where Rehoboam's decision to reject wise counsel led to the kingdom's division
options.B: Shechem was the most important city
options.C: Jeroboam forced the division against the people's will
options.D: The assembly had no real power

questions.explanation: Rehoboam's arrogance at Shechem triggered the irreversible split of the kingdom.

questions.question: How did Rehoboam's failure to listen reflect a broader principle of governance?

options.C: A wise ruler must balance firmness with understanding of his people's needs
options.D: All people's requests should be granted

questions.explanation: Rehoboam's refusal to hear justified complaints demonstrated poor leadership.

questions.question: What does the prophecy about Josiah burning the priests' bones indicate about divine justice?

options.B: God's judgment unfolds across generations, even centuries later

questions.explanation: The prophecy showed that God's judgment is certain but patient, accomplished through history.

questions.question: Why did God elevate Jeroboam if He knew he would become evil?

options.A: God tests faithfulness; even elevation comes with the possibility of choice and failure

questions.explanation: God gave Jeroboam opportunity to rule justly, but he chose idolatry.

questions.question: What distinguishes Asa's walk with God from preceding northern kings?

options.C: Whole-hearted devotion mirroring David, not half-hearted compromise

questions.explanation: Asa's loyalty to God's law paralleled David's and brought national blessing.

questions.question: How does Ahab's description as worse than all kings before him illustrate escalating evil?

options.B: Continued rejection of God's truth leads to deeper apostasy and more blatant sins
options.C: Evil rulers are always weak militarily
options.D: The worst rulers always face immediate judgment

questions.explanation: Ahab pushed idolatry further than any predecessor, even married to a Baal priestess.

questions.question: Why was Elijah's appearance suddenly declaring a drought God's response to idolatry?

options.A: Nature's judgment reinforces God's message when kings reject Him
options.D: The drought had no spiritual significance

questions.explanation: The drought was a sign that Baal, the storm god, had no real power.

questions.question: What does Elijah's ultimatum at Mount Carmel demand about covenant faithfulness?

options.A: Faith can be divided between multiple religions
options.D: Covenant with God demands exclusive allegiance; divided loyalty is impossible

questions.explanation: Elijah's challenge forced Israel to recognize that serving multiple gods is actually serving none.

questions.question: Why did the conversion at Mount Carmel prove temporary rather than lasting?

options.A: Elijah was not a true prophet
options.B: Miracles alone do not change hearts; Ahab's idolatry and Jezebel's opposition persisted
options.C: The people lacked sincerity at the moment

questions.explanation: The spectacular display changed minds temporarily, but did not transform the nation's heart.

questions.question: Why does God reveal Himself to Elijah through a still, small voice rather than mighty demonstrations?

options.C: True knowledge of God comes through intimate revelation, not just power displays

questions.explanation: The still, small voice teaches that faith requires listening and relationship, not just spectacle.

questions.question: Why was the reminder that God controls mountains and valleys relevant to Ahab's military situation?

options.A: It meant God's power extends everywhere; terrain provides no refuge from His will
options.B: It was a geographical lesson only

questions.explanation: The prophet assured Ahab that God's sovereignty transcends all geographical limitations.

questions.question: Why does Ahab's covetousness and Jezebel's solution reveal moral degradation?

options.A: Desire for property is natural and blameless
options.B: The king's inability to accept the word 'no,' combined with Jezebel's willingness to murder to satisfy him, shows complete moral collapse
options.C: Ahab was justified in wanting the vineyard
options.D: Property laws did not apply to kings

questions.explanation: Together, Ahab's unchecked desire and Jezebel's ruthlessness epitomize spiritual bankruptcy.

questions.question: How does Elijah's pronouncement of Ahab's judgment embody the principle of divine justice?

options.A: Punishment is arbitrary and without connection to sin
options.B: The victim is forgotten in divine judgment
options.C: God's justice is perfectly matched to the crime; Ahab's death mirrors Naboth's; consequences follow naturally from actions

questions.explanation: Elijah proclaims poetic justice: Ahab will meet the same fate as his innocent victim.

questions.question: Why did Ahab surround himself with false prophets rather than seeking true counsel from God?

options.A: A ruler committed to his own will seeks validation, not truth
options.C: False prophets are actually more reliable

questions.explanation: Ahab preferred comforting lies to uncomfortable truth—a sign of spiritual corruption.

questions.question: Why did Micaiah's honest prophecy about Ahab's defeat stand in such stark contrast to the other prophets?

options.B: True prophets speak God's word regardless of consequences; false prophets tell kings what they want to hear

questions.explanation: Micaiah's loyalty to truth over comfort distinguished him as God's true prophet.

questions.question: Over what territory did Solomon rule?

options.B: All the kingdoms from the river to the land of the Philistines to the border of Egypt
options.C: Only the lands of Judah and Benjamin

questions.explanation: Solomon's dominion extended across the vast territory promised to David.

questions.question: How many measures of wheat and barley did Solomon send to Hiram each year?

options.A: 20,000 measures of wheat and 20,000 measures of pure oil

questions.explanation: Solomon provided Hiram with a yearly payment of wheat and oil.

questions.question: Who was the chief craftsman Solomon hired to work on the temple?

options.C: Hiram, a widow's son from Tyre

questions.explanation: A skilled Hiram from Tyre oversaw much of the temple's construction.

questions.question: What did Solomon say no god compared to the LORD God of Israel?

options.B: In heaven above or on earth beneath

questions.explanation: Solomon affirmed God's unique and supreme nature.

questions.question: After Solomon finished praying, what did he do before all the congregation?

options.D: He arose and blessed the congregation

questions.explanation: Solomon rose and pronounced a blessing on all Israel.

questions.question: What does the description of people dwelling safely, each under his vine and fig tree, indicate?

options.A: Peace and prosperity during Solomon's reign

questions.explanation: This image represents security, abundance, and contentment under wise rule.

questions.question: Why did Solomon wait before building the temple?

options.A: He had no desire to build it
options.B: The kingdom was at peace and secure, making the time right for such a major project
options.C: He was afraid of the work

questions.explanation: Solomon correctly recognized that peace was a prerequisite for the project.

questions.question: What was inside the temple to serve the worshippers?

options.C: The golden altar, table for the showbread, and lampstands

questions.explanation: The temple contained the furnishings necessary for proper worship of God.

questions.question: What claim did Solomon make about the LORD dwelling in darkness?

options.A: The LORD said He would dwell in the thick darkness of the temple

questions.explanation: Solomon quoted the text indicating God's presence in the holy place.

questions.question: What would happen if Israel forsook God and turned to other gods?

options.B: They would be cast out of the land and the temple would become a byword among nations

questions.explanation: God warned of exile and destruction if the people were unfaithful.

questions.question: How did Solomon's heart become divided?

options.A: His wives turned his heart away to other gods
options.C: God allowed it for no reason

questions.explanation: Solomon's love for foreign women led him to idolatry.

questions.question: Who did the LORD raise up as an adversary against Solomon?

options.B: Hadad the Edomite and Rezon of Damascus

questions.explanation: God raised external enemies as discipline for Solomon's apostasy.

questions.question: What did Rehoboam's young advisors counsel him to say?

options.C: My little finger is thicker than my father's loins; I will add to your yoke

questions.explanation: The young men encouraged pride and harshness.

questions.question: What happened to Jeroboam's hand when he stretched it out against the man of God?
questions.explanation: Jeroboam's hand withered as immediate judgment for his hostility.

questions.question: What did Asa do that was right in the sight of the LORD?

options.D: He removed the idols and turned the people back to the LORD

questions.explanation: Asa's religious reforms and removal of idolatry pleased God.

questions.question: Who was Ahab's wife and what was her background?

options.A: A daughter of the Egyptian pharaoh
options.B: Jezebel, daughter of the king of the Sidonians and a devotee of Baal

questions.explanation: Jezebel brought Baal worship and moral corruption to the kingdom.

questions.question: Who brought Elijah his food during the drought?
questions.explanation: Ravens miraculously brought bread and meat each day.

questions.question: What did Elijah tell the widow to do, and what would result?

options.A: Leave her home and be blessed
options.C: First make a cake for him, then for herself; the flour and oil would not fail

questions.explanation: Elijah promised that her provisions would last if she first helped him.

questions.question: What did Elijah ask of God at Mount Carmel?

options.B: To answer by fire so the people would know the LORD is God

questions.explanation: Elijah called on God to demonstrate His reality through a visible sign.

questions.question: What happened to the prophets of Baal after the judgment?

options.C: They repented and became priests of God
options.D: They were slain at the brook Kishon

questions.explanation: Elijah executed the false prophets as judgment for leading Israel astray.

questions.question: What did Elijah do after receiving Jezebel's death threat?

options.A: Feared and fled for his life

questions.explanation: Elijah became afraid and fled into the desert.

questions.question: Who told Ahab he would defeat the Syrians?
questions.explanation: A prophet assured Ahab of victory through God's power.

questions.question: Who owned the vineyard that Ahab desired?
questions.explanation: Naboth owned the vineyard near Ahab's palace in Jezreel.

questions.question: What made Ahab especially wicked according to the text?

options.A: He sold himself to do evil, egged on by Jezebel
options.B: He was naturally evil from birth
options.C: He had no choice in his actions

questions.explanation: Ahab's willingness to be influenced by Jezebel into evil made him especially culpable.

questions.question: What did Ahab do during the battle where he was mortally wounded?

options.A: Led the charge against the Syrians
options.B: Remained in his chariot while pretending to be fighting

questions.explanation: Ahab stayed in his chariot but an arrow found him anyway.

questions.question: How does the period of peace under Solomon illustrate the reward of wisdom?

options.A: Wise leadership brings peace and stability, allowing prosperity and rest
options.B: Peace comes from military strength alone
options.C: Peace is accidental and not the result of leadership

questions.explanation: Solomon's early wisdom brought internal peace and external stability.

questions.question: Why is the completion of the temple significant beyond the building itself?

options.A: It was just a building project
options.B: It represented the fulfillment of David's vision and God's presence with His people
options.C: It marked the beginning of Israel's troubles

questions.explanation: The temple symbolized God's covenant relationship with Israel.

questions.question: Why does Solomon's acknowledgment in his prayer that all people sin show maturity?

options.C: He understood that even the righteous need God's forgiveness; the temple provides a means

questions.explanation: Solomon's prayer showed realistic understanding of human nature and God's mercy.

questions.question: Why does Solomon's building of temples to foreign gods represent the ultimate spiritual collapse?

options.A: It shows apostasy had reached its extreme; the builder of God's temple now built temples to idols

questions.explanation: Solomon's contradiction—honoring false gods—marks the nadir of his spiritual decline.

questions.question: Why was the cry 'To your tents, O Israel!' not merely political but spiritual?

options.B: It represented a break not just political but also spiritual, as the northern kingdom became prone to idolatry
options.D: It united the people in faithfulness

questions.explanation: The division set up the conditions for northern apostasy.

questions.question: Why was Elijah's declaration of drought without the king's permission an act of prophetic authority?

options.A: It showed disrespect for the king
options.C: It demonstrated that God's word through prophets supersedes royal authority
options.D: It was an act of rebellion

questions.explanation: Elijah's action proclaimed that God's authority transcends the king's.

questions.question: Why was the fire from heaven consuming even the water consuming the sacrifice so significant?

options.A: It showed God's absolute power, overcoming all obstacles, including the water barrier Elijah set up

questions.explanation: The fire's intensity and completeness proved God's sovereignty beyond question.

questions.question: Why did God commission Elijah to anoint Hazael and Jehu as king?

options.B: To bring God's judgment on the apostate kingdoms of Israel and Aram through political upheaval

questions.explanation: These anointed rulers would execute God's judgment on idolatrous kingdoms.

questions.question: Why did God delay judgment on Ahab despite his grave sins?

options.C: Ahab humbled himself; God's mercy delayed judgment to the next generation

questions.explanation: Even partial repentance moved God to extend mercy, though not to cancel eventual judgment.

questions.question: How does the fulfillment of Elijah's prophecy at the pool of Samaria show the certainty of God's word?

options.A: The dogs licked Ahab's blood exactly as Elijah prophesied, proving God's word never fails

questions.explanation: The exact fulfillment attested to the absolute truthfulness of God's prophetic word through Elijah.
